display.purpose: Page titles and member hero numbers. Geometric, tight tracking.

mono.purpose: Tabular numerics, member IDs, promo codes.

shadows.name: state-lift
shadows.purpose: Card on hover; rises -translate-y-1. Confirms interactivity.

shadows.name: signal-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary button at rest; intensifies to 0.40 on hover.

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: active: state on buttons; tactile feedback.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The signal action. One per view. Cyan gradient, brand-tinted glow, presses on click.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.625rem 1.25rem; background: linear-gradient(to right, #06b6d4, #0891b2); color: #fff; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; border: none; border-radius: 0.75rem; box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(6,182,212,0.25); transition: all 0.3s ease-out; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: linear-gradient(to right, #0891b2, #0e7490); box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(6,182,212,0.40); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(6,182,212,0.40); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Low-emphasis sibling to primary. Slate fill, 1px border, same press.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.625rem 1.25rem; background: #f1f5f9; color: #334155; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 0.75rem; transition: all 0.2s ease-out; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #e2e8f0; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Form field. White, slate border, cyan focus ring + border shift.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 0.625rem 1rem; background: #fff; color: #0f172a; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 0.75rem; transition: all 0.2s ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #06b6d4; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(6,182,212,0.40); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Pill reporting real state. Color always paired with text (color-blind safe).
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.25rem; padding: 0.125rem 0.625rem; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; } .ds-badge-success { background: #d1fae5; color: #047857; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Active = brand-tinted bg + brand text. Rounded, label type.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; padding: 0.625rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.75rem; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #475569; transition: all 0.15s ease-out; cursor: pointer; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #f1f5f9; } .ds-nav-active { background: #ecfeff; color: #0e7490; }

narrative.overview: NexoFitness is an operator's command surface. The person on the other side of the screen is running a gym, often with a queue at the front desk, and the interface exists to make their next action obvious and fast. Electric cyan is the signal color, used as a deliberate spotlight on a calm slate field, never as wallpaper. Hierarchy is carried by tone and spacing first; color second. A second identity layer rides on top: each tenant gets dynamic branding via --gym-brand on member-facing surfaces. Structure stays constant across tenants; the per-gym color does the branding work.
keyCharacteristics: Cyan is a signal, not a surface. One spotlight per view.
Slate neutrals carry hierarchy; dark mode is first-class.
One primary task per screen, loudest element on the page.
Mobile-first member surfaces; desktop is the adaptation.
Tenant color (--gym-brand) overrides cyan on public/member surfaces without changing structure.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Cyan (or the tenant --gym-brand) marks the single most important action or state in a view. If two things are cyan, neither is the signal.

rules.name: The Tenant Override Rule
rules.body: On any /public or /member surface, the brand color is var(--gym-brand), never hardcoded cyan. Structure stays identical; only the signal hue changes.

rules.name: The Sentence-Case Rule
rules.body: UI labels are sentence case. Reserve uppercase for <=4-word badges only. No tracked uppercase eyebrows above sections.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest; shadows appear only as a response to state (hover, focus, the primary signal).

dos: Do keep one cyan signal per view: the primary action or the most important state, nothing else.
Do drive all public/member branding through var(--gym-brand), never hardcode #06b6d4 on /public or /member surfaces.
Do carry hierarchy with tone + spacing first; reach for color second.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest and lift them on hover/focus with brand-tinted shadows.
Do use rounded-xl (0.75rem) and active:scale-[0.98] consistently so controls feel tactile.
Do ship a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every animation; body text >=4.5:1, large >=3:1.
Do pair every status color with an icon or label (color-blind safe).
donts: Don't build the generic SaaS template: no hero-metric cards, no identical icon-heading-text card grids, no tracked uppercase eyebrows or 01/02/03 markers.
Don't drift toward the loud consumer fitness app: no neon-everywhere, no gamified noise, no aggressive gradient backdrops.
Don't ship the dated enterprise admin: no gray Bootstrap tables, cramped rows, or flat hierarchy.
Don't clutter: one primary task per screen, no widget soup, protect breathing room.
Don't lean on text-gradient or default glassmorphism decoratively; a single solid color and weight contrast beats gradient text.
Don't use border-left/right > 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or alerts.
Don't let a second element compete with the cyan signal.
